DELAYED REPORT FROM MARCH 25. MICROBURST CONFIRMED IN VENANGO TOWNSHIP WITH WINDS OF 90-100 MPH. DAMAGE BEGAN JUST WEST OF PLANK ROAD WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED 10 TO 30 FEET ABOVE THE GROUND AND SCATTERED DEBRIS ACROSS PLANK ROAD BETWEEN MERRITTS LANDING AND ZIMMERMAN ROAD AND DAMAGE ENDED ON EAST BANK OF FRENCH CREEK. THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW OUT WINDOWS AND TORE OFF THE SHEET METAL ROOF OF A FISHING CAMP AND BLEW A TREE ON A HOME AND DEBRIS ON AN RV TRAILER. THESE STRUCTURES WERE LOCATED BETWEEN PLANK ROAD AND FRENCH CREEK. TREES AND DEBRIS FELL IN THE SAME DIRECTION, FROM WEST SOUTHWEST TO EAST NORTHEAST, INDICATIVE OF STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS.
REPORT FROM MPING: HALF DOLLAR (1.25 IN.).

ROOF OF BARN BLOWN ONTO RAILROAD TRACKS AND CAUSED TRAIN ENGINE TO DERAIL. HAY BARN DESTROYED AND TOPPLED STACKED ROLLS OF HAY. TREES SNAPPED OFF AND TOPPLED.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5 MILES ENE OF SYCAMORE AND LIFTED 4 MILES WEST OF CHATFIELD. THE TORNADO DAMAGED THE ROOF OF A BARN NEAR CR 100. AS THE STORM MOVED EAST, A LARGE DAIRY BARN WAS DAMAGED AND MOVED. FINALLY AS THE STORM PUSHED EAST, SILO BOLTS WERE RIPPED FROM CONCRETE AND PUSHED ONTO RAILROAD TRACKS. A TRAIN MOVING WEST COLLIDED WITH THE SILO. A TWO-CAR GARAGE WAS DAMAGED. A CONCRETE BLOCK WAS BLOWN UP INTO A TREE AND SEVERAL TWO BY FOURS WERE IMPALED INTO THE GROUND AND ROOF OF THE HOUSE. THIS DAMAGE WAS NEAR AND ALONG SR 19 NEAR THE WHEELING AND LAKE ERIE TRACK. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS ARE 85 MPH.

Preliminary NWS storm survey determined an EF-2 tornado with estimated peak winds of 130 mph and path width of 300 yards occurred in Lucas County, OH. The tornado touched down 5 miles north of downtown Toledo and tracked 2 miles east into Point Place. More details on damage to follow.
Multiple utility poles down and report of a roof blown off of a house in Washington Township. Time estimated via radar.
Numerous reports of trees snapped and structural damage, including roofs ripped off buildings, in Point Place. Possible tornado.
Numerous trees snapped. Minor damage to a vehicle reported as well as minor damage to a roof. Time estimated via radar.
Large hail reported in Maumee Bay State Park. Time estimated via radar.
Report from mPING: Tennis Ball (2.50 in.).
Numerous large trees snapped and damage to a building. Possible tornado damage.
Time estimated via radar.
Buoy station THRO1 Toledo, OH.
C-Man station THRO1 Toledo, OH reported a 57 kt wind gust.

Social media video post showed a waterspout northeast and north of the lodge over Maumee Bay. Waterspout developed over Maumee Bay and moved east southeast onshore as a tornado on the eastern part of Maumee Bay State Park.

NWS determined an EF-0 tornado with estimated peak wind of 80 mph and width of 50 yards. Social media posts showed a waterspout north of the lodge and damage to the boardwalk. Waterspout developed over Maumee Bay and moved onshore as a tornado on the eastern part of Maumee Bay State Park. Several tree, mostly dead, were blow over damaging the boardwalk.
Time estimated via radar.

Preliminary NWS storm survey determined an EF-2 tornado with estimated peak winds of 130 mph and a path width of 200 yards occurred south of Oak Harbor in Ottawa County, OH. The tornado was on the ground for approximately 9 minutes and tracked 3.45 miles to the east-northeast, damaging multiple homes and destroying several barns.
Damage to multiple houses near Hetrick and Bloom Roads. Possible tornado damage.
Damage to vehicles and farm buildings. Pictures via social media. Possible tornado damage.
Corrects time on previous report from 6 SE Oak Harbor. Emergency manager reported storm damage in Rice Twp. Possible tornado.
Emergency manager reported storm damage in Rice Twp. Possible tornado.

Several reports of structural damage along and around State Route 19 south of Oak Harbor in Ottawa County. Damage includes siding damage, outbuilding wall failure, and a roof blown off of a barn. Reports of trees down as well. Time estimated via radar.

An EF-1 tornado with estimated maximum winds of 95 mph and a width of 100 yards briefly touched down near the intersection of Township Road 153 and Township Road 146 in Rice Township, OH. The tornado blew down multiple trees, two of which fell onto a house. The tornado was on the ground for 0.15 miles.
Multiple trees snapped and powerlines down along the Turnpike/I-80. Time estimated via radar.

Delayed report. An NWS Cleveland storm survey confirms an EF-0 tornado briefly touched down in Sandusky County east of Vickery on June 15, 2023. The tornado touched down along CR 247 partially destroying a metal roof of an outbuilding and throwing roofing into a field. Tornado tracked east southeast for a third of a mile across Schertz Ditch and continued into a tree line. Shingles from a home were thrown back toward the west. The maximum width was 50 yards.
Outbuilding destroyed with debris scattered into field near US-6 and County Road 298. Time estimated via radar.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd dmg report from 2 WSW Sandusky. Poles snapped along US-6.
Poles snapped along US-6.
Barn collapse. Time estimated via radar.
Numerous trees and powerlines down.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd dmg report from Bellevue. Trees snapped along Northwest Street.
Trees snapped along Northwest Street.
A trained spotter reported golf ball sized hail near SR 269 and Lincoln Street in Bellevue.
Large trees downed just north of Bellevue. Time estimated via radar.

A preliminary NWS storm survey confirms an EF-0 tornado with estimated maximum winds of 80 mph and a width of 500 yards began in northern Bellevue. The tornado tracked 0.93 miles southeast and knocked over multiple power poles and snapped large branches off the tops of several trees before dissipating.

Updates previous tornado report from 2 S Monroeville. An NWS Cleveland Storm Survey confirms an EF-2 tornado with a maximum estimated wind speed of 115 mph occurred in Huron County on June 15 2023. This tornado started 2 miles south of Monroeville and extended 9.43 miles to 1 mile west-northwest of North Fairfield. The tornado had a maximum width of 1100 yards.
Trained spotter reported quarter sized hail in Bascom.

An NWS Cleveland Storm Survey confirms an EF-2 tornado with estimated maximum wind speeds of 115 mph in Huron County from northern Peru Township to Northwest of Greenwich. Additional details will be released in a Public Information Statement on Saturday.

An EF-0 tornado with max wind speed of 80 mph began at the intersection of Hettle Rd and Terry Rd where it uprooted a pine tree and snapped several large branches. This tornado tracked southeast and dissipated after crossing Peru Center Rd.
Multiple trees down north of Bellevue. Report obtained on social media and time estimated via radar.
Large tree uprooted. Time estimated via radar.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).
Golf ball sized hail between Bascom and Tiffin.
Damage to houses and outbuildings. Numerous trees and powerlines down. Possible tornado damage. Time estimated via radar.
Metal Barn partially destroyed on Ridge Road.

An NWS Cleveland Storm Survey confirms an EF-1 tornado with a maximum wind speed of 110 mph occurred in Huron County on June 15 2023. This tornado started 2 miles north of North Fairfield and extended 9.92 miles to 1 mile west-southwest of Greenwich. The tornado had a maximum width of 1100 yards.
A brief EF0 tornado began on State Route 61 snapping a pine tree, power lines, and causing additional tree damage as it tracked southeast.

Numerous trees and limbs snapped at Plum Brook Country Club. Cover also removed from outdoor pool and bar. Radar signatures show a divergent wind pattern characteristic of a microburst beginning over the golf course which led to a fanning out of tree damage that mostly landed towards the east or southeast...with two trees falling towards the north where the microburst began. Based on the amount of tree damage...nearby measured gusts...and radar data the wind gusts at the country club may have exceeded 70 MPH.

An NWS Storm Survey confirmed a weak, intermittent EF-0 tornado with a maximum estimated wind speed of 70 mph occurred in Holmes County on August 12, 2023. The tornado began near Township Road 501, south of State Route 39 and continued southeast to County Road 51 before dissipating. Tree damage consistent with a weak tornado was evident along this short, intermittent path, including a variety of uprooted and snapped trees and sheared off tops of other trees. No structural damage was observed and no injuries were reported.

An EF1 tornado has been confirmed beginning near South Nissen Road and West Deno Road where several tree limbs were broken off. The tornado continued southeast and damaged a nursery greenhouse. The tornado crossed the Portage River and blew down several trees and scattered broken limbs onto several homes. The tornado tracked southeast and uprooted three large trees onto a home and damaged the roof of this home. The tornado tracked southeast with max winds of 110 mph where several tree were blown down, damage to home siding, and snapped power lines along County Road 92. Max width was 100 yards.

An EF1 tornado has been confirmed in Mentor. The tornado began just west of Dartmoor Road near Mentor Avenue where large trees were downed. The tornado tracked east, downing numerous large trees. Multiple homes on Stockbridge Road and Middlesex Road were damaged when trees fell on them. In addition, damage to siding and roofs occurred at multiple homes. Extensive tree damage continued east across the Great Lakes Mall property before the tornado shifted northeast and caused additional tree and power line damage near the intersection of Johnnycake Ridge Road and Fairview Avenue. Further tree damage was observed in Wildwood Park before the tornado ended just south of Donald E. Kruger Park.

An EF-1 tornado has been confirmed southeast of Chardon. The tornado began near the intersection of Aquilla Road and Tewksbury Lane where it caused significant damage to trees and power lines before tracking southeast through the Meadow Wood Drive cul-de-sac. Extensive damage to trees, including numerous snapped and uprooted trees, occurred. From the end of Meadow Wood Drive, the tornado tracked due east, significantly impacted a property on the west side of Taylor Wells Road. Here, a swath of trees was snapped and uprooted across the property. The tornado lifted on the east side of Taylor Wells Road where additional tree damage was observed.
Tree down on house with Structural Damage - Mutual Aid to Munson along Meadowhurst Road. Large amount of debris on Auburn Rd.
Report from mPING: 3-inch tree limbs broken; Power poles broken. Time adjusted via radar.
Report of trees and limbs down on Heritage Woods Drive in Copley. Report via social media.
Tree downed across state route 91 at Stow Road in Stow. Time estimated via radar.

An EF-2 tornado has been confirmed in Middlefield. The tornado began near the intersection of Burton-Windsor Road and Route 608 where it caused minor damage to trees. It tracked southeast, quickly intensifying before it completely destroyed a barn and causing extensive damage to the property. The entire barn was lofted and thrown approximately 150 yards due southeast and the entire outer structure composed of cinder blocks collapsed. Adjacent barns received extensive damage as well. From this location, the tornado traveled south-southeast before lifting near the intersection of Route 608 and Nauvoo Road. Additional trees damage was located on that property.
Several trees down and displaced property at a residence on State Route 303 west of State Route 44.
*Delayed Report* Significant tree damage along Graham Road near Country Club Road in Silver Lake. Pictures from social media.
Tree down in front of Homestead Manor Trailer Park.
Tree down on a car in Goodyear Heights in Akron. Report via social media.
Six large trees down near State Route 43 and I-76. Additional limbs and branches were also reported down.
*Delayed Report* Significant tree damage and power lines down around Kent University. Pictures from social media.
Spotter 1.5 miles SW of Kent reported numerous trees and limbs snapped, power out, and a measured 80 MPH gust on a backyard weather station.
Report from Spotter Network of multiple limbs up to 6 inches in diameter downed along route 3 in Creston. Time estimated via radar.
Mesonet station GELO1 1 W Geneva-on-the-Lake.
Multiple tress and limbs down in Tuscora Park.
Damage to a property, including the collapse of a car port and damage to a roof.
*delayed report* Several trees snapped on Tallmadge Rd between Biltz Rd. and Kline Rd. Power lines also downed.

An EF-0 tornado has been confirmed in Bristolville beginning just east of Mahan Denman Road on Hyde Shaffer Road where large tree branches were snapped. The tornado traveled east, damaging corn fields and additional trees before crossing Thompson Clark Road. North of the intersection of Thompson Clark Road and Hyde Shaffer Road, multiple homes sustained minor damage to siding, shingles, and roofing and one home had the car port collapse. The tornado continued east, paralleling Hyde Shaffer Road before ending near North Park Avenue.
*delayed report* Numerous trees snapped near Stroup Rd and County Highway 18 in Edinburg Township. One tree fell on a barn.
Tree reported downed on a house in Wooster. Time estimated via radar.
Report from spotter network...multiple large trees and power lines down in Hartville, especially along N Prospect Ave. Time estimated via radar.
*Delayed Report* Detached multi-car apartment garage was destroyed. Pictures from social media.
Several trees snapped and uprooted and damage to a barn off McCallum Ave NE north of Price St NE.
Trees and power lines down on Scattell St NW, Elmvale St NW, Mapleview St NW, and Persimmon St NW.
*delayed report* Numerous trees are uprooted and snapped near Berlin Lake and in North Benton. There was also hail damage to cars.
Multiple trees down across the area.

An EF1 tornado has been confirmed in northeast Erie County OH. The tornado uprooted trees and broke off large tree limbs. Several limbs fell onto homes. One large limb partially destroyed a home near Mari-Dor Beach Cottages. The tornado tracked southeast into a rural area and lifted.
Tree down, partially on a house.
Tree on house.
Several trees blown down on S Broad Street and Fair Street in Canfield. Also a power line is down on Fairground Boulevard.
Numerous trees and power lines down. One tree on a house.
Numerous pine tree tops and large branches broken on New Buffalo Road just south of Western Reserve Road.
Tree down on road.

*delayed report* Several large tree limbs caused power outages in Mt Gilead. There was also a utility pole that was snapped at the base on the county courthouse campus. Several roads also had high water.
Mesonet station XCON Conneaut Light.
42 knot gust reported at Walnut Creek access area by trained marine spotters.
Tree downed along Hershey Road from outflow winds ahead of a thunderstorm.

At least 3 mature waterspouts between 715 am-737 am. Numerous other funnels also observed.
